name: NATCARB Saline Formations (US)
publisher: NETL/NATCARB
license: US Government work / public domain (verify EDX terms)
quality_notes: Pre-existing download from earlier session, not redone.

name: GLiM 0.5deg gridded lithology (Hartmann & Moosdorf 2012)
publisher: PANGAEA / Uni Hamburg
license: CC-BY (PANGAEA default)
quality_notes: Coarse 0.5deg raster aggregate, pre-existing download. Insufficient resolution for site-level work; full vector identified below but too large (>1GB) to download under size limit.

name: GLiM full-resolution vector (LiMW_GIS 2015.gdb)
publisher: Hartmann & Moosdorf / Uni Hamburg (hosted via Dropbox, linked from PANGAEA/CCGM.org)
license: CC-BY per Hartmann & Moosdorf 2012 GLiM terms (attribution required)
coverage: Global, 1,235,400 polygons, 16 level-1 lithological classes incl. vb (volcanic basic), pb (plutonic basic), py (pyroclastic), and further subclasses
quality_notes: VERIFIED WORKING DOWNLOAD LINK (confirmed via curl, resolves and downloads full 1.1GB .gdb.zip). Exceeds 100MB download-size guidance so not fetched in this session. RECOMMEND: download out-of-band, filter to classes vb/pb (and any ultramafic-tagged subclasses) in QGIS/ogr2ogr, then simplify/reproject before use. This is the best available source for the mineralization base layer; class-code legend needs to be pulled from the paper (Hartmann & Moosdorf 2012, Table 1) since not included in web page text.

name: PLATES Global Ophiolite Compilation
publisher: University of Texas Institute for Geophysics (PLATES project), compiled from Exxon tectonic maps (Mann & Taira 2004 methodology)
license: Not explicitly stated on site; UTIG PLATES data historically distributed for research/academic use — verify before commercial redistribution
quality_notes: Contains ophiolites.shp/.shx/.dbf/.prj/.sbn/.sbx, ~876KB shp. Coarse/generalized outlines from legacy tectonic maps digitization (not a modern detailed compilation) but only global open ophiolite GIS layer found. Good starting point for mineralization layer; may need manual supplementation for Oman/Samail and other well-studied ophiolites.

name: Geoscience Australia - Advanced CO2 Geological Storage Sites 2030
publisher: Geoscience Australia (Commonwealth of Australia)
license: CC-BY 4.0 International
coverage: Australia (Cooper Basin, Surat Basin QLD, offshore Gippsland Basin VIC)
quality_notes: Contains CCSBasins2030_AdvancedSitesAugust2021.shp + sidecar files. Covers only advanced-stage 2030 sites, not full basin prospectivity. Also available as WMS/MapServer: http://services.ga.gov.au/gis/services/AdvancedCO2GeologicalStorageSites2030/MapServer/WMSServer

name: Sedimentary Basins of the World (AAPG Datapages / CGG)
publisher: AAPG Datapages GIS Open Files / CGG
license: AAPG Datapages GIS Open Files terms - free download but check redistribution terms (CGG-sourced, historically restricted from commercial resale)
quality_notes: VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED: ArcGIS Hub page returned only title metadata via fetch (likely needs interactive ArcGIS Hub download or Esri REST API query, blocked from simple WebFetch). Direct shapefile export URL not resolved this session. Recommend follow-up via ArcGIS Hub API: https://hub.arcgis.com/api/v3/datasets/9845f1f30a1641efbe54dd1f9c8c668b/downloads or the item's FeatureServer query endpoint. CGG commercial dataset ancestry noted by user as proprietary/skip - but AAPG Datapages open-file mirror may be openly licensed; verify license text before using as primary injection basin layer.
recommended_action: verify license and try ArcGIS REST API export before including in production layer

name: EU CO2StoP Database (GEUS/JRC/EGDI)
publisher: GEUS, TNO, BGS for EC Joint Research Centre
url: https://setis.ec.europa.eu/european-co2-storage-database_en ; interactive atlas at https://maps.europe-geology.eu/ (EGDI); KML/CSV exports referenced at JRC
format: KML (spatial) + CSV (attributes) per JRC open conversion
license: EC JRC open data (verify per-dataset terms on EGDI)
coverage: Europe (EU member states + Norway/UK offshore where included)
quality_notes: VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED: JRC converted original ArcGIS/Access database to open KML+CSV format for non-commercial-software access, but direct file URLs not located this session (buried in setis.ec.europa.eu interactive portal / EGDI viewer, likely requires WMS/WFS query or manual portal navigation). RECOMMEND follow-up: query EGDI WFS service at https://maps.europe-geology.eu/ for CO2StoP storage unit layers, or contact GEUS for the KML export referenced in their 2014 and 2021 reports (data.geus.dk).

name: Ernst & Youbi (2017) Large Igneous Provinces (LIP) outlines, ed. Swanson-Hysell Group
publisher: Swanson-Hysell Group (UC Berkeley), modified from Ernst & Youbi 2017 for Park et al. 2020 LIPs-and-climate paper
license: No LICENSE file in repo; cite Ernst & Youbi (2017) and Park, Swanson-Hysell et al. (2020) Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3981262 for provenance/attribution
coverage: Global, 0-1300 Ma, both LIP source-region outlines and present-day extents
quality_notes: Downloaded LIP_Outline_edited.shp/.shx/.dbf/.prj/.qpj (main polygon dataset, ~800+ LIP source-region outlines, both Phanerozoic 0-520Ma and Proterozoic 520-1300Ma). Did NOT download the larger LIP_current_extent_plot_0_520.shp (~40MB present-day-extent version with more vertices) or the individual Franklin.shp/Umkondo.shp (tiny supplementary LIPs) - both available at same repo path if needed. Good primary source for flood-basalt/LIP polygons for mineralization layer.

name: USGS Data Series 414 - Ultramafic rocks of the conterminous United States (Krevor et al. 2009)
publisher: USGS (Krevor, Graves, Van Gosen, McCafferty 2009), with Earth Institute at Columbia University
license: US Government work / public domain
quality_notes: Contains US_ultra.shp - polygons of magnesium-rich ultramafic rocks suitable for mineral CO2 sequestration, compiled from 100+ regional geologic maps. Direct match for the mineralization-suitability layer over the US; DOI 10.3133/ds414.

name: USGS National Assessment of Geologic CO2 Storage Resources - regional SAU polygon shapefiles (OFR 2012-1024 series chapters, ScienceBase spatial-data releases)
publisher: USGS Eastern Energy Resources Science Center
url: ScienceBase items: ​66745eafd34e68d163086b2f (Ch.P: Appalachian/Black Warrior/Illinois/Michigan basins), 669165a0d34efe33cf415922 (Ch.R: Anadarko/S. Oklahoma basins). Full chapter index at https://pubs.usgs.gov/of/2012/1024/ (chapters a-r, each a separate regional report, most with their own ScienceBase spatial-data child item)
license: US Government work / public domain
coverage: United States, partial: Appalachian, Black Warrior, Illinois, Michigan, Anadarko, Southern Oklahoma basins captured this session
quality_notes: These are storage-assessment-unit (SAU) polygon shapefiles - the actual injection-target geometries, more useful than DS774 (which is tabular-only, no direct GIS polygons at that item). ScienceBase item search q='Carbon Dioxide Storage Resources Spatial Data' surfaces only 4 chapter items (P,Q,R,O) meaning most of the ~18 regional OFR 2012-1024 chapters (Gulf Coast, Columbia Basin, Williston Basin, Powder River Basin, San Juan Basin, etc.) have NOT yet been migrated to ScienceBase spatial-data child items, or use different naming - would need to check each chapter page https://pubs.usgs.gov/of/2012/1024/{a..r}/ individually for a 'GIS data' download link. California basins (Ch.Q: Central Coast, LA, Sacramento, San Joaquin, Ventura) and Wind River Basin (Ch.O) items exist on ScienceBase but their file-download URLs 404'd in this session (newer sciencebase.usgs.gov/manager storage backend, file paths may have moved) - VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED, retry recommended via ScienceBase item pages directly: https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/67c5c721d34ea599a3b991e1 and https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/65ca89c2d34ef4b119cb334b
recommended_action: Systematically walk chapters a-r at pubs.usgs.gov/of/2012/1024/{letter}/ to find remaining regional SAU shapefiles; retry California/Wind River ScienceBase file downloads.

name: Norway Sodir/NPD CO2 licence areas (FactMaps)
publisher: Norwegian Offshore Directorate (Sodir, formerly NPD)
license: Norwegian Licence for Open Government Data (NLOD) - Sodir/NPD data is open per Norwegian government data policy; verify exact terms at sodir.no
coverage: Norwegian continental shelf (North Sea, Norwegian Sea, Barents Sea)
quality_notes: 14 features - these are CO2 EXPLORATION/EXPLOITATION LICENCE polygons (administrative boundaries), NOT the geological storage-formation/aquifer extent polygons from the CO2 Storage Atlas. The Factmaps service (layer IDs enumerated via /MapServer?f=json) did not expose an obvious 'storage formation extent' or 'aquifer' polygon layer among its ~600+ layers - only CO2-storage wellbores (pt), CO2 licence areas (poly). RECOMMEND: the true storage-complex polygons are likely only published as static PDF maps in the CO2 Storage Atlas reports (sodir.no/en/whats-new/publications/co2-atlases/) and may need manual digitization, or check DataService/Themes folders under the same Factmaps REST root for a dedicated 'CO2 storage atlas' layer not yet located.

name: UK NSTA UKCS Carbon Storage Licences (ED50/WGS84/ETRS89)
publisher: North Sea Transition Authority (NSTA)
format: ArcGIS FeatureServer/MapServer (query as GeoJSON) / data.gov.uk listing
license: UK Open Government Licence (data.gov.uk listing implies OGL)
quality_notes: VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED: confirmed real, indexed REST endpoint via web search (data.nstauthority.co.uk/arcgis/rest/services/Public_ED50/UKCS_CarbonStorage_Licences_ED50/FeatureServer/0, polygon geometry, supports geojson output, max 1000 records) but the host data.nstauthority.co.uk did NOT resolve via DNS from this sandbox environment (curl: 'Could not resolve host', WebFetch: ENOTFOUND) - likely a sandbox network/DNS restriction rather than the service being down. Also note: like Norway, these are LICENCE areas, not necessarily the full CO2Stored geological storage-unit polygons (those require registered access at co2stored.co.uk, per project brief - confirmed still gated, email info@co2stored.org.uk for access). RECOMMEND: retry the FeatureServer query URL from an unrestricted network: https://data.nstauthority.co.uk/arcgis/rest/services/Public_ED50/UKCS_CarbonStorage_Licences_ED50/FeatureServer/0/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson

name: Queensland CO2 Geological Storage Atlas GIS (GGSS 2010)
publisher: Greenhouse Gas Storage Solutions (GGSS) for Queensland Government
license: Check data.gov.au listing (likely CC-BY per Queensland Government open data policy)
quality_notes: VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED: data.gov.au has migrated off the CKAN API to a Drupal-based portal (old CKAN API endpoint /api/3/action/package_show now returns the Drupal HTML shell, not JSON), and direct WebFetch on the dataset page also failed. Direct resource download URL not resolved this session. RECOMMEND: manual browser visit to https://data.gov.au/data/dataset/1321c7e2-805f-4434-9347-9e0181cfbce5 to find the resource download links.

name: Samail Ophiolite (Oman/UAE) georeferenced geologic map
publisher: D.B. Nothaft, University of Colorado Boulder, digitized from Nicolas, Boudier, Ildefonse & Ball (2000)
license: Cite Nothaft (digitizer) and Nicolas et al. 2000 (original map); check CU Scholar item page for explicit license (likely CC-BY given institutional repository norms)
quality_notes: VERIFIED-NOT-DOWNLOADED: WebFetch on CU Scholar item page returned 403 (likely requires JS-rendered download button / bot-blocking). Contains 5 lithology classes incl. Dunite/Wehrlite, Harzburgite, Mafic - exactly the peridotite/serpentinite-relevant units for in-situ mineralization. High-value target for the Oman ophiolite outline requested in the brief; RECOMMEND manual browser visit to https://scholar.colorado.edu/concern/datasets/zp38wd78m to get the direct .gpkg download link.

name: Macrostrat API (fallback query recipe for mafic/ultramafic units, not downloaded as bulk data)
publisher: Macrostrat / UW-Madison
url: https://macrostrat.org/api ; docs at https://v2.macrostrat.org/docs ; e.g. https://macrostrat.org/api/v2/units?lith_class=mafic&format=geojson or /geologic_units/map query with lith_class parameter
license: CC-BY 4.0 (Macrostrat data policy)
coverage: Global (best coverage US/Canada via merged state geologic maps)
quality_notes: NOT DOWNLOADED - documented as fallback recipe only, per task instructions. Macrostrat's /api/v2/geologic_units/map endpoint accepts lith_class or lith_type filters; can filter for 'mafic'/'ultramafic' lithology classes and export polygons as GeoJSON tile-by-tile (it's a map-tile-based API, not a single bulk download - would need scripted pagination across lat/lng tiles or use the burwell endpoint with a bounding box). Useful as a supplementary/gap-filling source where GLiM/LIP/ophiolite layers are sparse, particularly for well-mapped areas like the conterminous US.

name: BC Digital Geology (bedrock geology, incl. ultramafic/ophiolitic terranes e.g. Cache Creek Terrane)
publisher: British Columbia Geological Survey (BCGS)
format: GeoPackage or Esri Shapefile (per BCGS 'latest data release')
license: BC government open data - verify exact terms on BCGS download page (typically Open Government Licence - British Columbia)
quality_notes: VERIFY-ONLY per task scope (not downloaded). BC Digital Geology province-wide bedrock geology package includes lithology attributes and a QGIS/Esri style file for bedrock unit colours; would need filtering by lithology code for ultramafic/serpentinite units (e.g. Cache Creek Terrane ophiolitic rocks). No separate dedicated 'serpentinite layer' was found - it's a subset of the general bedrock geology polygons. Access route: https://www2.gov.bc.ca/gov/content/industry/mineral-exploration-mining/british-columbia-geological-survey/geology/bcdigitalgeology

name: SIGEOM Quebec geoscience data (bedrock geology, incl. serpentine/ultramafic units)
publisher: Ministere des Ressources naturelles et des Forets (MRNF), Quebec
format: File Geodatabase (FGDB), Shapefile, GeoPackage, CSV, or KML - user-selectable at extraction
license: Free extraction, no authentication required per MRNF documentation
quality_notes: VERIFY-ONLY per task scope (not downloaded). SIGEOM 'a la carte' tool lets users query by theme or NTS 1:50,000 map sheet and export in multiple GIS formats including SHP/GPKG, free and no login required. Would need a scripted or manual per-tile extraction filtered to serpentine/ultramafic lithology codes (Quebec has significant ultramafic belts e.g. Thetford Mines ophiolite, Asbestos region). Access route confirmed working (no auth barrier), but bulk province-wide single-file export not confirmed - likely requires tiling by NTS sheet or region-of-interest polygon.

name: USGS World Petroleum Resources Project 2009-2011 - Province/TPS/Assessment Unit Boundaries (WEP_AU)
publisher: USGS (World Energy Program)
format: File Geodatabase (.gdb), 2 layers: WEP_AU (global, non-US) and CARA_AU (Circum-Arctic Resource Appraisal 2008)
license: US Government work / public domain
coverage: Global (non-US) priority petroleum provinces incl. Middle East/N.Africa, Africa, S.America, Asia-Pacific, FSU, Europe, Canada (Alberta Basin); plus separate Arctic layer covering Russia/Norway/Canada/US Arctic shelves
local_path: data/raw/basins/usgs_world_petroleum_2009-2011_province_tps_au.gdb.zip (raw gdb), extracted to data/raw/basins/extracted_wep2009/DDS69ff.gdb/; dissolved province-level outputs at data/raw/basins/usgs_wep_2009-2011_provinces_dissolved.geojson (153 provinces) and data/raw/basins/usgs_cara_2008_arctic_provinces_dissolved.geojson (32 Arctic provinces)
quality_notes: THIS IS THE GLOBAL BACKBONE LAYER. The originally-targeted 2000 World Petroleum Assessment 'Geologic Provinces of the World' ScienceBase items (60ad2fd7d34e4043c850edb3 'all defined provinces' and 60ad2fa1d34e4043c850ed98 'assessed provinces only') were VERIFIED but their actual shapefiles are MISSING from ScienceBase - only thumbnail JPGs remain (files appear to have been lost during a platform migration); their WFS/WMS mapping endpoints at sciencebase.gov/catalogMaps also 404. Found this more-recent (2013 data release, from the 2009-2011 international assessment cycle) replacement instead, discovered via ScienceBase full-text search. WEP_AU has 270 assessment-unit polygons across 152 unique provinces (join field: PRV_NAME / PRV_CODE); dissolved to 153 province-level polygons (one extra due to a name recorded under two PRV_CODEs). Confirmed major basins present: Rub al Khali Basin, Niger Delta, Santos Basin, Songliao Basin, Bohaiwan Basin (=Bohai Bay), North Sea Graben/Anglo-Dutch Basin (North Sea), Alberta Basin (=WCSB Canadian portion), Zagros Fold Belt, West Siberian Basin. GAPS: no Permian/Illinois/Williston (US - covered separately, see Sedimentary_Basin USA layer below); no Cooper Basin (Australia) or Indus Basin - need supplemental regional sources for these. CARA_AU (Circum-Arctic Resource Appraisal, 2008) adds 70 AU / 32 dissolved provinces for Arctic Russia/Norway/Canada/Alaska shelves, join field PROV_NAME/PROVCODE.
recommended_action: Use PRV_NAME (WEP_AU) and PROV_NAME (CARA_AU) as the basin-name join keys. Consider re-attempting the legacy 2000 'all defined provinces' dataset later via a USGS FOIA/data-release request or the Wayback Machine (no working snapshot with GIS data found this session - only PDF regional report bundles at pubs.usgs.gov/dds/dds-060/regions/reg[1-8].zip, which contain zero shapefiles, only report PDFs).

name: USGS Sedimentary Basins of the United States (Coleman & Cahan 2012, OFR 2012-1111)
publisher: USGS Central Energy Resources Science Center
url: https://energy.usgs.gov/arcgis/rest/services/BaseMaps/Sedimentary_Basin/MapServer/0 (queried live as GeoJSON); report at http://pubs.usgs.gov/of/2012/1111/
license: US Government work / public domain
coverage: United States (all onshore + offshore/EEZ basins, incl. Alaska and Hawaii)
quality_notes: 144 named basin polygons, fields: nogaprvnce, area_acres, perimet_mi, plttectset (plate tectonic setting), era, basintype, source, source_scl, name, basin_num. Confirmed presence of Illinois Basin, Permian Basin, Williston Basin, Michigan Basin, Appalachian Basin, Gulf of Mexico Basin, Anadarko Basin. This is the best available single US-wide basin catalog (companion/superset relative to the piecemeal USGS OFR 2012-1024 SAU shapefiles already in usgs_sau_regional/ - those are storage-assessment-unit level within a subset of these basins, useful as a finer join once basin names match).

name: Australian Geological Provinces (2018.01 edition)
publisher: Geoscience Australia (Commonwealth of Australia)
url: https://services.ga.gov.au/gis/rest/services/Australian_Geological_Provinces/MapServer/22 (AllProvinces layer, queried live as GeoJSON, paginated in batches of 25); item page https://amsis-geoscience-au.hub.arcgis.com/maps/geoscience-au::australian-geological-provinces-1/about
format: GeoJSON (queried from live ArcGIS MapServer, geometry simplified with geopandas .simplify(0.005 deg, preserve_topology=True) to shrink from 148MB raw to 6.3MB)
license: CC-BY 4.0 International (Commonwealth of Australia / Geoscience Australia 2018)
quality_notes: 516 total province polygons (406 tagged type='sedimentary', plus igneous/metallogenic/tectonic provinces mixed in - filter on properties.type=='sedimentary' for the basin layer). Fields: provinceid, name, altname, type, subtype, rank, parentname, state, onoffshore, source. CONFIRMED PRESENT: Cooper Basin, Surat Basin, Gippsland Basin, Bowen Basin, Perth Basin, Canning Basin, Bonaparte Basin, Northern/Southern Carnarvon Basin, Otway/Inner Otway Basin - this closes the Cooper Basin / Australia gap left by the USGS WEP_AU global backbone layer (which has no Australian provinces) and is a major upgrade over the previously-held ga_advanced_co2_storage_sites_2030.zip (which only covered 3 advanced-stage 2030 sites, not full basin prospectivity). NOTE: the live MapServer query endpoint returned intermittent HTTP 500 errors at larger batch sizes (100) around certain offsets - resolved by using batch=25; if re-querying, use small batches with retry logic.
recommended_action: Join on 'name' field (matches common basin names like 'Cooper Basin', 'Gippsland Basin'). Use properties.type=='sedimentary' to filter to basin-relevant polygons only.

name: ANP Brazil Sedimentary Basin Sectors (SETORES_TODOS_SIRGAS)
publisher: Agencia Nacional do Petroleo, Gas Natural e Biocombustiveis (ANP), Brazil
license: Brazilian federal open-data (ANP dados abertos); no explicit CC license text found on page but distributed as public open data by a federal regulatory agency
coverage: Brazil, all onshore + offshore sedimentary basins
local_path: data/raw/basins/anp_brazil_bacias_setores_sirgas.zip (raw shapefile), extracted to data/raw/basins/extracted_anp_brazil/; dissolved to basin level at data/raw/basins/anp_brazil_basins_dissolved.geojson (36 basins)
quality_notes: 188 sector polygons (exploratory-model sub-divisions within each basin) with fields ID1, BACIA (basin name), NOME_SETOR (sector name). Dissolved by BACIA to 36 basin-level polygons. CONFIRMED PRESENT: Santos, Campos, Pelotas, Potiguar, Recôncavo, Sergipe_Alagoas, Espirito Santo_Mucuri, Parnaíba, Solimões, Amazonas, Foz do Amazonas, Paraná - closes the Brazil gap; note the direct .zip URL returns HTTP 403 to default curl (no User-Agent) but succeeds with a browser User-Agent header set.
recommended_action: Join on 'BACIA' field for basin-level matching (Portuguese names, e.g. 'Santos' = Santos Basin, 'Sergipe_Alagoas' = Sergipe-Alagoas Basin - matches WEP_AU global layer's 'Santos Basin' naming after normalization).

name: USGS Coal-Bearing Regions and Structural Sedimentary Basins of China and Adjacent Seas (OFR 2000-047)
publisher: USGS
format: Shapefile (no .prj; coordinates confirmed geographic/WGS84-compatible by bounding box, CRS manually assigned EPSG:4326)
license: US Government work / public domain
coverage: China and adjacent seas (mainland China, Taiwan, Korea, Japan margin basins)
local_path: data/raw/basins/usgs_china_sedimentary_basins_of00-047.zip (raw), extracted to data/raw/basins/extracted_china_basins/; cleaned CRS-assigned GeoJSON at data/raw/basins/usgs_china_sedimentary_basins.geojson
quality_notes: 43 basin polygons, fields AREA, PERIMETER, AGE_BASIN_ (code), NAME, ERA. CONFIRMED PRESENT: Songliao Basin, Bohai Bay Basin, Sichuan Basin, Tarim Basin, Ordos Basin, Junggar Basin, Qaidam Basin, Zhujiangkou (Pearl River Mouth) Basin - closes the China gap. A handful of trailing features (South Bijia Basin, North/South Shuangfeng Basin x3 duplicated each, 2 unnamed None) look like small supplementary/offshore fragments, harmless for a name-join.

name: Nyberg & Howell (2015) global sedimentary basins classification
publisher: N/A - not found
url: https://doi.org/10.1130/G36669.1 (paper only, no linked GIS repository found)
quality_notes: SEARCHED, NOT FOUND: no GSA Data Repository item, Zenodo/PANGAEA/Dryad record, or author-hosted GIS file located for this paper's basin classification/outlines. The maps in the paper appear to exist only as static figures, not a distributed shapefile. Not pursued further - superseded in value by the USGS WEP_AU 2009-2011 backbone and regional supplements already acquired.
recommended_action: Skip; if a GIS version is later needed, contact authors (Nyberg, B. and Howell, J.A., Uni Research/University of Bergen) directly.

name: AAPG Datapages / CGG Sedimentary Basins of the World (re-verification)
publisher: CGG Robertson via AAPG Datapages / ArcGIS Hub
license: EXPLICITLY RESTRICTED per Hub item licenseInfo: "made available publicly for academic, research, industry or personal use. The dataset may not be used in whole, or in part, within any dataset for resale or distribution without written permission from CGG." Also: item is currently returning HTTP 403 'Subscription is canceled, the item is not accessible' from the ArcGIS Online sharing API - the hosting org's subscription appears to have lapsed.
quality_notes: CONFIRMED SKIP per task instructions (CGG/Robertson = proprietary). License text now directly confirms redistribution restriction, and the item is presently inaccessible even for viewing/export due to an ArcGIS Online subscription lapse on the publisher's end.
recommended_action: Do not use in the atlas; license forbids redistribution/resale-adjacent use.
